text.title: Plausible Analytics Setup Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Plausible Analytics Setup. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Plausible script loaded exactly once per page, with `defer`, in `<head>`. Multiple loads inflate counts.
All conversion goals declared in Plausible UI before the script ships, not retro-added (avoids historical-data gaps).
rule.rationale: Goals added post-hoc miss the period under measurement.

rule.statement: Custom properties carry segments / plans / referrers — never email, name, IP, user-id.
rule.rationale: PII in props turns Plausible from privacy-first into a regulatory liability.

rule.statement: Script served from same-domain subdomain (analytics.example.com) or proxy. Direct plausible.io is ad-blocker prone.
rule.rationale: Direct CDN load is blocked by uBlock + Brave by default; counts undercount.

rule.statement: Each external stakeholder gets a scoped shared-link, not the master account credentials.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-plausible-analytics.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
